title: [InstCombine] Split GEPs with multiple variable indices
summary: The generalization strategy transforms a specific GEP pattern into a reusable optimization by splitting multi-variable-index GEPs into sequential single-variable-index GEPs. Initially, the optimization handled only a single test case with constant offsets. The patch introduces a systematic check: after processing GEP-of-GEP folding, it counts variable indices and prevents creating GEPs with more than one variable index. Then, in the main GEP visitor, it iterates through indices, detects the second variable index, and splits the GEP into two parts: the first GEP uses all indices up to (but not including) that second variable index, and the second GEP uses a zero constant followed by the remaining indices. This preserves semantics while enabling further simplifications (like constant folding) on each part. The approach is robust across various scenarios—different types (arrays/vectors), address spaces, inbounds flags, and vector GEPs—by leveraging existing GEP construction utilities and maintaining no-wrap flags. Developers can apply similar strategies by identifying multi-variable operations that block optimization and splitting them into simpler, optimizable sequences.
